observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our audit recommendations in CY 2021 with modification that
the Municipal Accountant:
a. maintain a complete and updated PPE and Real Property Ledger Cards to
ensure that all PPEs are properly accounted for and to easily facilitate the
reconciliation of the RPCPPE with accounting records;
b. Recognize the depreciation expense of the PPEs referred to in Table 1 and
henceforth, ensure that all depreciable PPEs are provided with
depreciation expenses;
c. Record the transfer of PPE totaling ₱21,484,484.48 from Trust Fund books
to its respective PPE classification in the General Fund books;
d. Reclassify as inventories the items with acquisition costs that are below the
capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00;
e. Identify the acquisition costs of items recorded in aggregate and reclassify
them as inventories if their acquisition costs are below the capitalization
threshold of ₱15,000.00 and henceforth record the procured assets
individually taking into consideration their individual acquisition costs;
f. Reclassify the incorrectly recorded PPEs to their proper account;
g. Reconcile the PPELC with the RPCPPE wherein differences noted should be
verified and the corrections be made, where necessary; and
h. Determine the cost of the various donated equipment and recognize them as PPE
in the books.

We also reiterated our previous recommendations, that the Municipal General
Services Officer:
a. Submit to the Municipal Accountant the documents of the donated properties
cited in Table 7 for proper recording in the books;
b. Issue Property Acknowledgment Receipts for donated equipment and Inventory
Custodian Slips for items below the capitalization threshold in lieu of the
previously issued Property Acknowledgment Receipt; and
c. Ensure that Report of Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) was prepared and
accomplished completely when the physical count is concluded.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal
Accountant at the beginning of each year a duly certified list showing the name
of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which will serve
as basis of the latter in the booking up of the RPT and SET Receivable
accounts at the beginning of each year.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant undertake periodic reconciliation of the balances per master list
of RPT delinquencies with that of the RPT and SET Receivable account
balances per books to come up with a more accurate and proper balance of
RPT and SET Receivable accounts.

We further recommended that the Municipal Assessor update the Assessment
Roll and reconcile all records pertaining to real properties within the
Municipality and its owners.

Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up the
necessary adjusting/correcting entries in the books on the advance payments
of RPT and SET Receivables collected during 2022. Likewise, he should follow
the correct accounting entries regarding advance payments of real property
and special education taxes to reflect the correct amount of RPT and SET
Income, Due to LGU’s, and other affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant together with the Municipal
Treasurer confirm the validity and existence of the outstanding liabilities of the LGU
for the noted stale checks and inform or send notices to the payees concerned
informing them of the status of the issued checks, if warranted.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a. prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant a monthly Schedule of
Unreleased Checks; and
b. reissue checks for payment to identified payees of the stale checks after
confirmation of the existence of the noted liabilities.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Promptly obtain the monthly bank statements from the depository bank and
regularly prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and ensure
submission of the same to the Audit Team;
b. Prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to restore the unreleased checks
based on the schedule prepared by the Municipal Treasurer at the end of
every year to fairly present the Cash in Bank balance of the LGU in the
Financial Statements, and to reverse the entries every January of the
ensuing year; and
c. Prepare adjusting journal entries to restore or derecognize the liabilities of
the LGU to the creditors/payees of the noted stale checks after
confirmation of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Conduct verification, analysis, and validation of the balances of the Due to
NGA accounts, determine the source agencies and the status of programs,
projects and activities for which funds were received, and maintain
complete records of the same;
b. Reconcile the balances of the account and effect the necessary adjustments,
if warranted, and prepare Liquidation Reports or FURs for fund transfers
already used for its intended purpose;
c. Recognize the corresponding asset or expense account upon utilization or
disbursement of funds; and
d. Henceforth, recognize income only upon the submission of liquidation
reports or FUR to the Audit Team and Source Agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the agency, require the
General Services Officer to prepare the monthly report of fuel
consumption (per Appendix G of COA Circular No. 77-61) and to submit the
reports to the Audit Team for audit purposes.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all driver personnel
of the municipality to accomplish the Driver’s Trip Tickets and prepare the
report of official travel on a monthly basis and to submit the same to the
General Services Officer for the preparation of the monthly report of fuel
consumption.

We further recommended that the BAC, thru the BAC Chairman:
a. prepare the Purchase Requests and the BAC Resolutions indicating the Approved
Budget of the Contract for the fuel, oil, and lubricant to be procured to avoid
incurrence of fuel expenditures beyond the approved appropriation of the LGU;
and
b. stop the practice of preparing procurement documents after the incurrence of
transactions and ensure strict adherence to the procurement process to avoid
disallowance in audit.

Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant stop the practice of
recording immediately to expense accounts the inventories procured/received by the
municipality. Recognize the corresponding expense account based on the monthly
Report of Supplies and Materials Issued, supported by duly approved RIS.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record all the transactions
of the municipality using the appropriate accounts provided in the Revised Chart of
Accounts for LGUs so that transactions are faithfully and correctly presented in the
financial statements which will provide reliable data to the users thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the cost of
registration and membership fees for seminar/conference/convention to the
Training Expense and Membership Dues and Contributions to Organizations
accounts instead of the Traveling Expense account pursuant to The Revised
Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer obligate the
payment for registration and membership fees to the Training Expense and
Membership Dues and Contributions to Organizations appropriation
accounts and henceforth ensure that all funds of the LGU were utilized in
accordance to the specific purpose for which they were appropriated.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer record all donations-in-
kind received by the RHU in the Summary/List of Donations Received,
Distributed and Balances, duly supported with the (a) Acknowledgment
Receipts, (b) proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries not
necessarily in the required form, and (c) the inventory of remaining
undistributed items, if any; and submit a copy of the same to the Supply
Officer and the Municipal Accountant.

We also recommended that the Supply Officer:
a. assist the Municipal Health Officer in the inspection and acceptance of all
 donations-in-kind, and in the conduct of inventory of the remaining
 undistributed items, if any; and
b. maintain copies of the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed
and Balances and its supporting documents and prepare and maintain a
Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.

Finally, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant, based on the Registry
of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM, make a disclosure in the Notes to FS as to
the quantity received, issued, and balance of all donation-in-kind received by
the Municipality, with categorization as to food items, medicines and medical
supplies, hygiene kits, and others.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the Municipal
Development Council, through the Municipal Planning and Development
Officer:
a. Identify and prioritize projects which are considered vital and urgent to
 the Municipality and include in the AIP only those priority projects which
 are ready for implementation in the current budget year to ensure optimal
 utilization of the 20% DF; and
b. Review the unimplemented projects in prior years funded from the
 Continuing Appropriations of 20% DF during budget preparation to
determine its viability and necessity for inclusion in the AIP, otherwise,
 recommend reversion of the funds if no longer needed.

We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
a. Conduct a detailed engineering of all projects prior to its inclusion in the
 AIP which includes the preparation of the Program of Work with the
 Detailed Estimates and plans/specifications;
b. Fast-track the implementation of priority projects identified for CY 2022
 and in the subsequent years; and
c. Submit to the Bids and Awards Committee the Program of Work with the
 Detailed Estimates, plans/specifications and Purchase Request within the
 timetable indicated in the AIP, taking into consideration the period within
 which the procurement process and project implementation will be
 completed.

We further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee schedule the
procurement of projects in accordance with the timeframe specified in the AIP
to ensure completion of the projects before the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal DRRM
Officer:
a. submit a written justification of the LGU’s action of allowing the
incurrence of non-disaster related expenditures and training expenditures
beyond the approved appropriation per LDRRMFIP; and
b. refrain from using the LDRRMF for activities and programs which do not
support disaster risk management and instead, utilize the LDRRMF
strictly for disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness,
disaster response, and disaster rehabilitation and recovery purposes only.

We also recommended that the Municipal DRRM Officer together with the
Municipal Budget Officer strictly monitor and evaluate the use and
disbursement of LDRRMF so as not to exceed the budgeted appropriation.

This audit observation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommended that the Accountable Officers concerned submit within 30
days from receipt of the AOM, the liquidation reports and the supporting
documents, otherwise, restitute/return immediately the amount equivalent to
their respective unliquidated cash advances.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Stop the grant of cash advances to officials and employees with outstanding
cash advances;
b. Issue a directive to the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries of
officials and employees who failed to liquidate their cash advances, based
on the recommendations of the Municipal Accountant; and
c. file the request for authority to write off the dormant unliquidated cash
advances to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising
Auditor (SA) after exhausting all possible remedies by management to
demand liquidation of cash advances.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Demand from the accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances,
the immediate settlement, refund or submission of liquidation reports on
cash advances where the purposes have already been served;
b. Recommend to the Municipal Mayor the withholding of salaries of
the accountable officers who continue to fail to submit their Liquidation
Reports within the period prescribed by regulations;
c. Prepare the schedule of dormant unliquidated cash advances by
accountable officer/debtor/government entity and by account, to support
the request for write-off, after exhaustion of all possible remedies by the
Management to demand liquidation of cash advances; and
d. Discontinue processing DVs for grants of additional cash advance to
officials and employees with unliquidated previous cash advances to avoid
accumulation thereof.

Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer withhold the salaries
of officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances, as directed by the
Municipal Mayor.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget
Officer submit a written explanation or justification regarding the deficiencies
above noted.

We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant:
a. Require all End-User Units to submit all documents for the procurement
of goods charged from the current year appropriation, the obligations of
suppliers having been duly fulfilled, based on the contract, during the
current year, to facilitate the recording of the expenses in the books of
accounts of the Municipality; and
b. Record all valid claims chargeable against the appropriations in the year
the expenses were incurred in the books of accounts of the Municipality
and recognize payables for all unpaid expenditures as of yearend.

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer initiate the
enactment of an Ordinance to make available the unexpended appropriation
which were reverted to the surplus of the general fund should there still be
payment of prior year’s unpaid obligations not recorded as payables, to avoid
the erroneous charging of prior years’ expenditures to the current year’s
appropriation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Head of the Human
Resources Management Office:
a. issue written guidelines on the standard procedures to be followed in the
hiring of JOs to ensure professional and impartial selection process;
b. revise the format of JO contracts to indicate the specific task to be
performed by each JO hired, the authority to undergo official travel in
relation to exigency of duty including the allowable expenditures to be
reimbursed for the official travel conducted, and other required terms and
conditions of the contracting parties;
c. limit the hiring of JOs only to the most necessary and only to complement
the existing workforce of the LGU;
d. limit to the most necessary the practice of allowing Job Orders to attend
conferences, seminars, and trainings and stop the grants of per diem claims
for travels undertaken in adherence to EO No. 77; and
e. submit a written justification of the LGU’s action of allowing Job Order
personnel to attend trainings, seminars, meetings, and other similar
activities even without being stipulated in their contract as well as the grant
of travel allowances (per diem) in relation to their travels in CY 2022.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Head of the Human
Resources Management Office formulate measures to address the problem of
delayed release of wages for JO personnel. Deadlines for the submission of
DTRs and Accomplishment Reports should be set and attention of JO
personnel causing the delay should be called and warnings may be made, if
warranted.

1.22 We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant to:
a. obtain the monthly banks statements from the depository bank regularly for all
depository bank accounts and prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation regularly
and submit the same to the Audit Team;
b. maintain a Subsidiary Ledger for each bank account that comprises the Cash in
Bank – LCCA account;
c. prepare an adjusting entry for the unrecorded fund transfer received amounting to
P13M;
d. restore the unreleased checks at the end of the year by preparing a journal entry
voucher and at the start of the ensuing year, draw another entry to reverse the
previous entry made and recognize the availability of the checks for release.
e. coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the difference of P802,807.52
and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries.

2.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
f. enforce liquidation of cash advances from officials/employees who are still
in service;
g. issue demand letters to all officials/employees with overdue accounts, copy
furnish the Audit Team and enforce pertinent sanctions as provided for
under COA Circular No. 2012-004;
h. ensure that employees with unliquidated cash advances shall not be
granted another cash advance in accordance with the provisions of Section
89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 to avoid accumulation of
unliquidated cash advances;
i. exert all possible means to trace back records/documents relative to the
unaccounted cash advances; if the liquidation of cash advances is no longer
possible due to the death of officials/employees, request write-off as
prescribed under Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005.

3.24 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
i. provide depreciation on the PPE referred to in paragraphs 3.12, 3.13
and 3.14;
ii. prepare entries to recognize donated equipment from the Province of
Leyte and DOH;
iii. record the AJE prepared for the transfer of PPE from Trust Fund
books to the General Fund;
iv. derecognize the items costing below the capitalization threshold of
P15,000.00 and recognize the same as prior period adjustment;
v. prepare adjusting entries to reclassify the incorrectly recorded PPE to
its proper account.
vi. Prepare entries to record the result of the reconciliation between the
GSO’s records and that of the Accounting Office.

3.25 We also recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer:
i. submit the documents on the donated properties to the Municipal
Accountant for proper recording in the books;
ii. ensure updated RPCPPE with complete information and unit value.
iii. issue a Property Acknowledgment Receipt for donated equipment and
Inventory Custodian Slip for items below the capitalization threshold
in lieu of the previously issued Property Acknowledgment Receipt.

4.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:
j. transfer immediately the remaining unexpended balance of LDRRMF
amounting to P3,217,633.31 to the Trust Fund books;
k. Submit explanation to the Audit Team why the entire amount was not
transferred to the Trust Fund books.

4.9 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit immediately the
remaining funds to the depository account with the DBP.

4.10 We also reiterate our recommendation that the LDRRM Officer should:
l. initiate the immediate transfer of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF
to the special trust fund account; and
m. include in the LDRRMFIP for every calendar year, under separate
caption, the list of projects and activities charged to prior years
unexpended LDRRMF.

5.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
n. submit to the Audit Team within five (days) upon receipt of this AOM the
lacking documents and a written explanation why the disbursement
vouchers were approved despite the deficiency; and
o. henceforth, review the completeness of the supporting documents of the
disbursement vouchers before forwarding these to the Municipal Mayor
for approval.

6.12 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the officials and
employees concerned to submit the lacking reports and documents to the
Audit Team for review and evaluation within 5 calendar days upon receipt of
this AOM, otherwise, a Notice of Suspension shall be issued.

6.13 Henceforth, ensure that Disbursement Vouchers are supported with the
required documents before these are forwarded to the Municipal Mayor for
approval.

7.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and other officials and employees concerned to submit to the
Audit Team the lacking appropriate documents presented in Table 17 within
5 days from receipt of this Memo. This is without prejudice to the issuance of
a Notice of Suspension/Disallowance, if warranted.

7.6 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit explanation for
the Audit Team’s evaluation why disbursement vouchers were approved for
payment despite the lacking documents. Henceforth, ensure that all
disbursements should be supported with complete documentations in
accordance with the provisions in PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated
June 14, 2012.

8.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit justification why
payment was made to these claims despite the deficient and lacking supporting
documents.

8.8 We also recommended that the BAC Chairperson and the BAC Secretariat
submit immediately the lacking contract documents within five (5) days from
receipt of this AOM. This is without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance, if warranted.

8.9 Moving forward, we recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee submit
original copies of contract documents and shall exert their best effort that all
supporting documents to the contracts are completely filled out and compliant
with the requirements of Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated
February 12, 2009 and Section 17.1 of the RIRR of RA 9184 and submit these
to the Audit Team within five (5) days after perfection of the contract.

9.7 We recommended that the Disbursing Officers concerned apply for an
increase amount of Fidelity Bond equivalent to at least 75% of their respective
cash accountability to ensure that all funds are within the coverage of the
Fidelity Fund of the government or limit the cash advance up to the maximum
cash accountability.

10.9. We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Design Engineer and the
Municipal Engineer should ensure inclusion of Project Billboard/Signboard
and Occupational Safety and Health as separate pay items in every
infrastructure project implemented in compliance with COA Circular No.
2013-004 dated January 20, 2013 and DPWH Department Order No. 56 s. 2005
dated April 20, 2005.

10.10. We also recommended that Project Billboard/Signboard be set up at the
project site for all the projects of the Municipality in a standard format so that
the public is duly notified of the essential features/elements of the project.

10.11. Further, we recommended that compliance on Construction Safety and Health
Program shall be required on all winning bidders to ensure that the workers
of LGU projects are adequately protected against occupational risks.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjustments in the books, through the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to re-classify the equipment and motor vehicles purchased in CY 2019 to the appropriate PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Submit to the Sangguniang Bayan the proposed amendments on Section 8, Article IV of the Municipal Ordinance No. 28-2010, specifically on the Penal Provisions, to clarify the conflicting sections on penalties to registered and unregistered stallholders;

Revise the contents of the Contract of Lease to conform with the Municipal Ordinance and any amendments thereto; and

Execute a Contract of Lease, with the authority from the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan, with all stallholders occupying properties owned by the Municipality. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:

Collect immediately the rentals, including the imposition of the fines and penalties, from all delinquent stallholders;

Monitor strictly the payments of rental fees of each stall holders; and

Issue Demand Letters after 3 months non-payment and implement Closure Order, if unpaid.

We recommended that the BAC, through its Chairperson, and the BAC Secretariat:

Prepare all the requisite documents when conducting competitive bidding to prove that each and every stage of the procurement are being strictly complied at all times; and

Submit to the Audit Team, all contracts and its complete supporting documents, regardless of mode of procurement, within five (5) days after approval by the Municipal Mayor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) identify the specific public infrastructures acquired prior to CY 2013; (2) record the same to the appropriate Infrastructure Asset accounts; (3) maintain property ledger cards for each public infrastructure, and; (4) prepare a lapsing schedule to compute depreciation of each asset, to afford its fair presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: On-going identification of infrastructures which were acquired in CY 2013

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), through the BAC Secretariat, post all Notices of Awards, Notices to Proceed and Approved Contracts in the PhilGEPS and LGU website in addition to the postings in at least three conspicuous places reserved for the purpose.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the above-cited governing rules and regulations in the recording and conduct of physical inventory of property, plant and equipment and shall specifically undertake the following:

require the Accounting and Supply Office to maintain the respective PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards and  regularly reconcile their records pertaining to PPE balances to prove, that the assigned values to the assets per the agency’s books are reliable and accurate and to afford fair presentation of the related PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer started to maintain the report on the current purchases, including its reconciliation, but they are still in the process of determining the previous balances

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should provide depreciation for the Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment account and the Water Supply System account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare a journal entry to restore the amount in the books. Henceforth, he should prepare the appropriate journal entries to record reconciling items to put the accounts in order.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer should secure the turn-over documents of the donated building from the DPWH.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare a journal entry voucher to record the donated building as soon as the documents are available.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit the lacking procurement documents and justification why payments were made despite the lacking documents.
Henceforth, management should comply with pertinent requirements before payment is made to avoid audit suspensions.

We recommended that the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer, in coordination with the Brgy. Officials should conduct identification of low-income families of each barangay of the municipality and obtain reliable data for inclusion in the municipality’s database so that a listing of eligible beneficiaries is always ready.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to return back to the DSWD the ₱5,000.00 returned from the beneficiary.

We recommended that the BAC should submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents for evaluation and an explanation why these documents were not submitted together with the contract or Purchase Order to the Audit Team within the reglementary period
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit justification for the Audit Team’s evaluation why payment was made even if procurement documents were not attached to the Disbursement Voucher.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit written justification for the Audit Team’s evaluation why these transactions were approved even without the required documents.
We recommended that henceforth, the LDRRM Council should:
initiate the immediate transfer of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF to the special trust fund account; and
include in the LDRRMFIP for every calendar year, under separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged under the unexpended LDRRMF from previous years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should:
Require the Municipal Health Officer to prepare and maintain the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and other required supporting documents on all in-kind donations received by their Office;
Require the General Service Officer to affirm and confirm the receipt and distribution of the in-kind donations including the maintenance of the related record; and
Require the Committee of Physical Inventory to include in-kind donations received by the Municipality in the conduct of physical count and the preparation of the related inventory report.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan enact a Resolution for the reactivation of the current depository accounts for proper disposition of its remaining funds such as the immediate transfer of the funds to the General Fund to avoid more from bank charges.

We recommended that the Vice Mayor should stop the practice of serving meals and snacks during the sessions in the SB Office, particularly during those activities which are considered regular or normal and to exercise prudence in utilizing government funds which are primarily intended for public purpose.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Maintain subsidiary ledgers for each component of the Road Network account and constantly update the same to support the balances reflected in the financial statements;

Prepare Journal Entry Vouchers to correct the erroneous entries on the partial payments of road projects in the amount of ₱2,888,020.61; and

Compute the depreciation of each component of the Road Network account from its initial recognition and in subsequent years, including the accurate computation of its estimated useful life without providing for any residual value, and reflect the amounts in the Accumulated Depreciation account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjustments in the books through the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to:

transfer to the General Fund books the cost of vehicles and equipment purchased from Trust Fund and record each in the proper PPE account; and,

correct the errors in recording and henceforth, use the Chart of Accounts as guide in recording transactions of the LGU.

We recommended that, in the subsequent preparation of Financial Statements, the Municipal Accountant present the current portion of Loans Payable account as a separate line item under Current Liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position for a better understanding on the financial status of the Municipality and provide the necessary information for budgetary purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Record the collections from the sale of Bid documents and any procurement-related disbursements under Other Payable account; and 

Review the General Ledger of Bail Bonds Payable to ascertain the correct balance of the account and prepare an adjusting entry on the misclassification of accounts totaling to 273,500.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Department Head in charge of the different projects and programs funded by various National Government Agencies, complete its implementation in accordance with the schedule in the MOA to expedite the realization of their intended purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the HRMO:

Revise the format of JO contracts to indicate the specific tasks to be performed by the JOs being hired, and other required terms and conditions of the contracting parties;

Require JOs to submit quantified accomplishment reports, certified by the Head of Office to where they are assigned, to support payments of wages; and

Limit the hiring of JOs only to the most necessary and only to complement the existing workforce of the Municipality.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer continue the speedy implementation of all the projects in the AIP to ensure the full utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Solid Waste Management Board:

Comply and integrate all the data needed by the EMB, as listed in its Evaluation Report, and resubmit its MSWMP to EMB for endorsement and approval by the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC); and 

Implement fully the strategies and activities embedded in the Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the said Committee to submit the Report on Physical Count of Local Road Network with the complete description and cost segregation of each component., and;

Require the Inventory Committee to provide the Accountant and the General Services Officer with a copy of the Report on Physical Count of Local Road Network for reconciliation with their respective records.

We also recommended that the General Services Officer maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Cards and Road Map for all the municipal roads and reconcile the same, at least once a year, with the records maintained in the Accounting Unit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor assist the Municipal Treasurer in the enforcement of collections from all delinquent tenants of wet and dry market stalls, otherwise, order the closure of said stalls until the accounts are fully paid by the tenant concerned.

We also recommended for the Members of the Sangguniang Bayan review the existing Municipal Tax Code and if necessary, formulate an updated Tax Ordinance to provide policies on stall rentals and on water operations including the adoption of actions to be taken  in case of non-payment of bills.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor propose, and the Sangguniang Bayan approve, amendments on the existing Municipal Ordinance on the operation of its Waterworks System to update the provisions thereon and improve the collection of necessary revenues for the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Maintain a Subsidiary Ledger for EACH bank account that comprise
the Cash in Bank account;
b. Prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank
accounts, and close inactive accounts;
c. Prepare immediately the adjustment in the books of accounts for the
erroneous entry made on the transfer of Loans worth ₱29,671,875.00
and other reconciling items in the BRS; and
d. Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile and tally the
balances between the Cashbook and the General Ledger and prepare
the required adjustments oi the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Trace back the transactions pertaining to all funds received from
National Government Agencies based on source documents and the
records maintained by the previous bookkeeper and reconcile with the
General Ledger balance;
b. Prepare the correct adjusting entries on the unrecorded and erroneous
recording of fund transfers and disbursements with net amount of
₱20,185,745.91;
c. Prepare Subsidiary Ledgers for EACH fund transferred from
different National Government Agencies and monitor its
disbursements and balances; and
d. Supervise closely the work of the new personnel to avoid errors in
recording all transactions of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Maintain subsidiary ledgers for each component of the Road Network
account and constantly update the same to support the balances
reflected in the financial statements;
b. Prepare Journal Entry Vouchers to correct the erroneous entries on
the partial payments of road projects in the amount of ₱2,888,020.61;
and
c. Compute the depreciation of each component of the Road Network
account from its initial recognition and in subsequent years, including
the accurate computation of its estimated useful life without providing
for any residual value, and reflect the amounts in the Accumulated
Depreciation account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary
adjustments in the books, through the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to re-
classify the equipment and motor vehicles purchased in CY 2019 to the
appropriate PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary
adjustments in the books through the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to:
a. transfer to the General Fund books the cost of vehicles and equipment
purchased from Trust Fund and record each in the proper PPE
account; and,
b. correct the errors in recording and henceforth, use the Chart of
Accounts as guide in recording transactions of the LGU.

We recommended that, in the subsequent preparation of Financial
Statements, the Municipal Accountant present the current portion of Loans
Payable account as a separate line item under Current Liabilities in the
Statement of Financial Position for a better understanding on the financial
status of the Municipality and provide the necessary information for
budgetary purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Record the collections from the sale of Bid documents and any
procurement-related disbursements under Other Payable account; and
b. Review the General Ledger of Bail Bonds Payable to ascertain the
correct balance of the account and prepare an adjusting entry on the
misclassification of accounts totaling to 273,500.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Department Head in
charge of the different projects and programs funded by various National
Government Agencies, complete its implementation in accordance with the
schedule in the MOA to expedite the realization of their intended purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Submit to the Sangguniang Bayan the proposed amendments on Section
8, Article IV of the Municipal Ordinance No. 28-2010, specifically on
the Penal Provisions, to clarify the conflicting sections on penalties to
registered and unregistered stallholders;
b. Revise the contents of the Contract of Lease to conform with the
Municipal Ordinance and any amendments thereto; and
c. Execute a Contract of Lease, with the authority from the Office of the
Sangguniang Bayan, with all stallholders occupying properties owned
by the Municipality.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a. Collect immediately the rentals, including the imposition of the fines
and penalties, from all delinquent stallholders;
b. Monitor strictly the payments of rental fees of each stallholders; and
c. Issue Demand Letters after 3 months non-payment and implement
Closure Order, if unpaid.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the HRMO:
a. Revise the format of JO contracts to indicate the specific tasks to be
performed by the JOs being hired, and other required terms and
conditions of the contracting parties;
b. Require JOs to submit quantified accomplishment reports, certified by
the Head of Office to where they are assigned, to support payments of
wages; and
c. Limit the hiring of JOs only to the most necessary and only to
complement the existing workforce of the Municipality.

We recommended that the BAC, through its Chairperson, and the BAC
Secretariat:
a. Prepare all the requisite documents when conducting competitive
bidding to prove that each and every stage of the procurement are
being strictly complied at all times; and
b. Submit to the Audit Team, all contracts and its complete supporting
documents, regardless of mode of procurement, within five (5) days
after approval by the Municipal Mayor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer
continue the speedy implementation of all the projects in the AIP to ensure
the full utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Solid
Waste Management Board:
a. Comply and integrate all the data needed by the EMB, as listed in its
Evaluation Report, and resubmit its MSWMP to EMB for endorsement
and approval by the National Solid Waste Management Commission
(NSWMC); and
b. Implement fully the strategies and activities embedded in the Plan.

The Municipal Accountant was not yet provided with the report while the report was still on-going preparation. observations.managementAction: Unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund from prior year were already included in the LDRRM Investment Plan.

observations.managementAction: Review of the projects, securing all Memorandum of Agreement entered into by the Municipality.

observations.managementAction: Review of the Tax Ordinance was conducted and pending for review. Water collection has minimal increased due to complaints of insufficient water supply.

observations.recommendation: require the Accounting and Supply Office to maintain the respective PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards and including the regular reconciliation of its balances pertaining to PPE to prove, that the assigned values to the assets per the agency’s books are reliable and accurate and to afford fair presentation of the related PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Preparation of the PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, including the regular reconciliation of its balances pertaining to PPE.

observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) identify the specific
public infrastructures acquired prior to CY 2013; (2) record the same to the
appropriate Infrastructure Asset accounts; (3) maintain property ledger cards
for each public infrastructure, and; (4) prepare a lapsing schedule to
compute depreciation of each asset, to afford its fair presentation in the
financial statements.

2.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately prepare the
monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts which shall be
submitted to the Audit Team. For an accurate Cash in Bank – LCCA
account balances, regular adjustment of said accounts shall be done
whenever errors in recording are disclosed in the monthly prepared BRS.

3.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1.) Create an Inventory Committee, with the Municipal Engineer as
Chairman, who shall conduct a thorough physical count of local road
networks of the LGU;
2.) Require the said Committee to submit the Report on Physical Count of
Local Road Network with the complete description and cost segregation
of each component., and;
3.) Require the Inventory Committee to provide the Accountant and the
General Services Officer with a copy of the Report on Physical Count of
Local Road Network for reconciliation with their respective records.
3.7 We also recommended that the General Services Officer maintain a complete
Local Road Network Property Cards and Road Map for all the municipal
roads and reconcile the same, at least once a year, with the records
maintained in the Accounting Unit.

4.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the appropriate
adjusting journal entries to record the receivables from the different
associations and require each recipient to submit a Liquidation Report on
how the funds were used..
4.10 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal
Agriculturist immediately execute a Memorandum of Agreement between
LGU of Villaba and the respective Association to establish accountability on
the transferred funds, monitoring and inspection of the project
implementation is being done, to ensure it was utilized for the purpose it was
granted.

5.10 We recommended that the Municipal DRRMO:
1.) Include in the annual LDRRMF Investment Plan, in a separate caption,
the list of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended
LDRRMF in previous years in the Trust Fund so that no separate
approval from the SB will be required;
2.) Implement all programs, projects and activities listed in the current year
LDRRMFIP to strengthen the capacity of the LGU to manage and
prepare prior to occurrence and during disasters.

6.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Bids and Awards
Committee and the Municipal Engineer, procure and implement all
identified priority projects in the AIP within the current year.
6.9 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal
Development Council include only in the AIP and utilize the 20% DF for
projects which are capital in nature.

7.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor assist the Municipal Treasurer
in the enforcement of collections from all delinquent tenants of wet and dry
market stalls, otherwise, order the closure of said stalls until the accounts are
fully paid by the tenant concerned.
7.13 We also recommended for the Members of the Sangguniang Bayan review
the existing Municipal Tax Code and if necessary, formulate an updated Tax
Ordinance to provide policies on stall rentals and on water operations
including the adoption of actions to be taken in case of non-payment of bills.

8.8 We recommended that the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC), through the BAC Secretariat, post all Notices of Awards, Notices to
Proceed and Approved Contracts in the PhilGEPS and LGU website in
addition to the postings in at least three conspicuous places reserved for the
purpose.

9.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board and the SWM Focal Person, specifically
undertake the following:
a) Follow-up the approval of 10-year SWMP so that the LGU could
immediately take the necessary actions to deficiencies noted if any;
b) Immediately start the procurement process for the Garbage Compactor
once the budget for CY2019 is approved in order to support the collection of
waste materials from the barangay;
c) Assist the 35 barangays to establish their own Materials Recovery Facility
to facilitate the segregation of solid waste at the barangay level;
d) Coordinate and require all barangays located in the mountainous areas to
have a proper solid waste management and closely monitor their
implementation;
e) Prioritize the provision of funds for the immediate establishment of a
complete processing facility which includes the acquisition of a Plastic
Shedder and Plastic Melter that will convert non-biodegradable materials
into useful products in order to attain the objectives of the LGU’s Ten-year
Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare journal entries
to record the donations from IFRC to correct the understatement of the assets and
its corresponding effect on the government equity account. Also, record all
subsequent donations immediately upon receipt of the funds or property.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Municipal Vice-Mayor
and the Members of the Sangguniang Bayan ensure that the Annual Budget of the
Municipality is approved within the prescribed period so as not to hamper the
social, economic and environment growth of the Municipality for the benefit of its
constituents.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal
Engineer, implement all identified priority projects in the AIP during the year to
maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and ensure that the funds
will be utilized on projects which will promote development of the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Municipal Vice Mayor
and Members of the Sangguniang Bayan, facilitate the early approval of the
Annual Budget of the LGU within the prescribed period in order not to hamper the
implementation of important programs, projects and activities that will capacitate
the LGU in addressing the needs of the community in times of disaster.

We recommended that the Municipal DRRM Council include in the
LDRRMF Investment Plan, in a separate caption, the list of projects and activities
to be funded from the LDRRMF in the Trust Fund to ensure its approval by the
legislative body. We also recommended that the MDRRMO implement only those
PPAs included in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Chairman and
Members of the BAC and the Municipal Agriculturist to fast track the
procurement of the remaining agricultural equipment and supplies for YRRP
program to afford its completion and immediate distribution to the identified
farmers’ association.

We also recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist, being the frontline
official of DA-funded projects, should always secure and retain a copy of the
Memorandum of Agreement for easy reference and guide in the project
implementation, monitoring and evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor propose, and the Sangguniang
Bayan approve, amendments on the existing Municipal Ordinance on the operation
of its Waterworks System to update the provisions thereon and improve the
collection of necessary revenues for the LGU.

We also recommended that the MENRO who is in- charge with the Water
System operations, assign meter readers in different barangays every week to
prevent connivance and familiarity with the consumers as a way of strengthening
the internal control of the LGUs waterworks system.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board, the Municipal Budget Officer and the Chairman and
Members of the BAC to undertake the following:

a) Prioritize the provision of budget for the acquisition of Plastic Shredder &
Plastic Melter which will be used to convert plastic materials into useful
products and place the biodegradable waste in a compost pit to clean up the
scattered garbage in the dumpsite;
b) Provide a budget for the construction of the building where the said
equipment will be mounted;
c) Fast track the acquisition of bulldozer and backhoe which will be utilized to
rehabilitate and improve the dumpsite;
d) Assist the 35 barangays to establish their own Materials Recovery Facility by
providing financial assistance in order to facilitate the segregation of solid
waste at the barangay level;
e) Coordinate and require all barangays located in the mountainous areas to
have a proper solid waste management and closely monitor their
implementation; and
f) Consider hiring personnel to fill-up the Environmental Specialists II item
who will be assigned as Solid Waste Management Focal Person in order to
ensure proper and effective implementation of Solid Waste Management
Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the created VMCWC
to perform actively their assigned functions and prepare annually the following
documents for submission to the Municipal Budget officer:

a.) LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan (AWFP) prioritized according
to needs for integration in the local development plan;
b.) Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC);
c.) Local Investment Plan for Children (LCC).

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer allocate funds in
its Annual Budget for the implementation of programs identified by the VMCWC
based on its AWFP.

We recommended that the Local School Board Council incorporate in its
budget programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care Development.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record procured medicines and supplies in the appropriate inventory account upon purchase to recognize in the books the asset and record the related expense upon its issuance for fair presentation of the financial statements. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Office maintain Subsidiary Ledgers and Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards respectively, for each type of inventory item, to monitor receipts and issuance and reconcile the respective balances monthly.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the provision of funds for the immediate acquisition of additional equipment necessary for the delivery of social services in terms of collection and disposal of waste and ensure the full implementation of SWM Projects integrated in the 20% development fund in order to attain the objectives of the LGU’s Ten-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan.
