observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the above-cited governing laws and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and shall undertake the following actions: a) Send demand letters to all accountable officers requiring urgent liquidation or settlement of outstanding cash advances failure of which should cause the withholding of salaries and other claims due them equivalent to the noted unsettled amount in case such demand is unheeded. b)The Municipal Accountant shall always maintain subsidiary ledgers for each accountable officers & employees who were granted cash advances to facilitate audit and verification on the accuracy of the reported balance of unliquidated cash advances. c)The Municipal Accountant shall ensure before crediting the account Due from Officers & Employees that it is supported with liquidation reports together with the required supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR Partially implemented.
observations.reason: findin g no. 1

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the relevant governing rules and regulations in the recording and conduct of physical inventory and specifically undertake the following: a) Create an inventory committee who will conduct a thorough physical count of PPE including their classification to the appropriate accounts and the assignments of the correct unit value to afford reconciliation of balances maintained in the Accounting Section and the Supply Office, the segregation of those that are no longer serviceable by class or type. b) Cause the immediate submission of inventory report on physical count of PPE to the concerned Audit Team for audit verification and validation; c) Require the Accounting and Supply Office to maintain the respective PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards and regularly reconcile their records pertaining to PPE balances to prove, that the assigned values to the assets per the agency’s books are reliable and accurate and to afford fair presentation of the related PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR Partially implemented.
observations.reason: findin g no. 2

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management particularly instruct the Municipal Treasurer to undertake the following measures: a) Impose penalty and apply the civil remedies provided in the pertinent Section of the Municipal Tax Code to delinquent tenants of market stalls and business taxpayers; b) Conduct examination of the books of accounts especially on delinquent business taxpayers to ascertain, assess and collect the true and correct amount of tax due from the taxpayer.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR Partially implemented.
observations.reason: findin g no. 3

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of the revised IRR of R.A. 9184 particularly in resorting to alternative mode of procurement , in this case, the Shopping mode and shall undertake the following corrective measures: a) Procurement thru the alternative mode, in this case Shopping, must be supported with a BAC Resolution indicating the reason why the resort to the said alternative mode duly approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity. b) Always support procurement thru Shopping of commonly use supplies and equipment with PS Certificate of non-availability of stocks to prevent the transactions from being disallowed in audit. c) Procurement of supplies, materials and equipment more than the prescribed threshold of P50,000.00 , the invitation to bid must be posted in the PhilGEPS copy of which must be attached to the disbursement vouchers to serve as proof of postings. Likewise, the certification from the BAC Secretariat the said procurement opportunities were posted at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the procuring entity for seven (7) calendar days.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR Implemented.
observations.reason: findin g no. 6

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the immediate refund by the concerned nine (9) Sanggunian Bayan Members of the respective amount alleged to the claimed attendance to the aboved-mentioned PCL National Congress and meeting.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR Partially implemented.
observations.reason: findin g no. 9

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adhere to the pertinent laws, rules and regulations governing Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) and specifically undertake the following: a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to regularly submits the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF using the format in Annex B of COA Circular 2012-002 to the COA Audit Team of the LGU; b) Annually prepare the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) which shall present the 30% QRF and 70% preparedness and mitigation fund; and c) Stop sending Job Order personnel to attend seminars and trainings charged against the LDRRMF or to any fund of the LGU since, they are not permanent employee and can be terminated anytime hence, cannot contribute to the improvement of agency operations.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR Implemented.
observations.reason: findin g no. 10

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of COA rules in the recording of purchased supplies and materials and equipment and to undertake the following: (a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO to strictly adhere to the established rules and regulations in the recording of the procured supplies and materials and equipment and to the maintenance of the prescribed Supplies Ledger Cards per inventory item and PPE ledger cards and Property Cards per PPE category with reconciled balances. To book up to the appropriate PPE account the procured equipment which were debited to the expense account to have the asset existing in the books of the agency and to afford fairness in their presentation in the financial statements as at a given period; (b) Instruct the Municipal GSO to prepare Purchase Request for purchases of supplies and materials based on the needed supplies and materials for the quarter of the respective departments per approved Annual Procurement Plan; (c) Instruct the Municipal GSO and Accountant to have all issuances of supplies and materials covered by Requisition Issue Slips (RIS) which will support the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) to be prepared weekly and submitted to the Accounting Office as basis for the recording of the supplies and materials issued; and d)Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to have all procurement for equipment charge to the approved budget for Capital Outlay of the agency.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 AAR Implemented.
observations.reason: findin g no. 2 CY 2011

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record procured medicines and
supplies in the appropriate inventory account upon purchase to recognize in the books
the asset and record the related expense upon its issuance for fair presentation of the
accounts in the financial statements.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services
Office maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards respectively, for each type of
inventory item, to monitor receipts and issuance and reconcile the respective balances
monthly.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare appropriate adjusting
journal entries to correct the understatement of the assets and the overstatement of the
expense accounts. Also, make sure that subsequent similar transactions are recorded
in the proper account for fair presentation of the related accounts in the financial
statements.

We recommend for the Municipal Accountant to provide and record the
depreciation charges on depreciable properties pursuant to IPSAS 17, and COA
Circular Letter No. 2004-003 dated October 4, 2004.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor designate a focal person to obtain
from the funding agencies copy of the MOA and other necessary documents to guide
the LGU in its project implementation.

Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the
Liquidation Reports and other documents to the source agencies including the return
of unexpended balances to facilitate succeeding release of funds for the program.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned Heads of
Offices to fast track the implementation of the projects. Also, to ensure that all
identified priority projects in the AIP are implemented during the year to maximize
the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and achieve desirable socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain Subsidiary Ledgers
for the 20% development fund to account all disbursements and balances of the fund
and provide information as to available balance at any given time during the year.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
transfer the unexpended balance of the CY 2015 LDRRMF amounting to
P4,511,801.03 to the Trust Fund and make such transfer every end of each year.

Also, the Municipal DRRMO include in the LDRRMF Investment Plan, in a
separate caption, the list of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended
LDRRMF in previous years in the Trust Fund.

We also recommend that both the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant
submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the concerned offices
and within the period prescribed by regulations.

We recommend that the MDRMO base the LDRRMFIP on the approved
budget for LDRRMF in order to allocate sufficient funds for disaster-related activities.
Also, to submit an explanation why the priority projects indicated in the LDRRMIP were not
implemented.

Also, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all identified
priority projects are implemented during the year to maximize the utilization of the
LDRRMF and to enhance the capability of the municipality on disaster preparedness.

We recommend that the Municipal School Board, through its Chairman,
include as topmost priority the improvement of educational facilities to maximize the
purpose of the Special Education Fund and ensure that optimum benefits for the
schools is achieved.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the lacking
documents and ensure that subsequent transactions are supported with complete
documents before signing box A of all DVs.

We recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, through
the MENR Officer, initiate the procurement of additional equipment for collection of
waste materials, the construction of Materials Recovery Facility, the conduct of
seminars and workshops in the barangay level and sustain the effective
implementation of the LGU’s Solid Waste Management Plan.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the provision of funds
for the immediate acquisition of additional equipment necessary for the delivery of
social services in terms of collection and disposal of waste and ensure the full
implementation of SWM Projects integrated in the 20% development fund in order to
attain the objectives of the LGU’s Ten-year Ecological Solid Waste Management
Plan.

We recommend that the Chairman of the BAC and the BAC Secretariat
strictly observe the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12,
2009, on the submission of copies of perfected contracts/purchase orders to the
Auditor within five (5) days after execution/issuance thereof, to afford timely review
and evaluation of the contract before the transaction is consummated.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the pertinent laws, mandates under Section 89 of PD 1445, COA Circular 97-002 and other relevant rules in related rules and regulations specially in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and liquidation of cash advances and the following shall specifically undertake the following courses of actions:
observations.managementAction: Conducted meetings
observations.reason: Still with unliquidated cash advances as at year end.

observations.recommendation: a) Send demand letters to all officials and accountable officers requiring urgent liquidation or their settlement of outstanding cash advances to facilitate audit and verification on the accuracy of the reported balance of unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemen ted. Conducted meetings
observations.reason: Still in the process of updating the records to support the outstanding unliquidated cash advances failure of demand letter to be issued.

observations.recommendation: b) The Accounting and the Supply Office shall maintain the respective Subsidiary Ledgers & records for each accountable officers and employees who were granted cash advances to facilitate audit and verification on the accuracy of the reported balance of unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemen ted. The Accounting and the Supply Office have started maintaining the respective PPE ledger cards and records for newly procured equipment and will exert effort in retrieving the records of the existing equipment.
observations.reason: The accuracy of the reported unliquidated balance of P2,413,198.45 as of December 31, 2015 cannot be ascertained with incomplete liquidation documents.

observations.recommendation: c) Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure before crediting the account Due from Officers & Employees that it is supported with liquidation reports together with the required supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemen ted. The Accounting Office started maintaining records and advised Municipal Treasurer to submit required liquidation documents.
observations.reason: There are still incomplete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: a) Create an Executive Order committee who will conduct a thorough physical count of PPE including their classification to the appropriate accounts and the assignment of the correct unit value to afford reconciliation of both records maintained in the Accounting Section and the Supply Office, the segregation of those that are no longer serviceable by class or type.
observations.managementAction: Not implemen ted. Creation of the inventory committee has been done but creation still unable to conduct thorough physical count committee.
observations.reason: An Executive Order was issued on creation of the inventory committee but still unable to conduct thorough physical count.

observations.recommendation: b) Cause the immediate submission of inventory report on physical count of PPE to the concerned Audit Team for audit verification and validation;
observations.managementAction: Designated GSO was instructed for a physical count of PPE.
observations.reason: No Report of Physical Count of PPE was submitted to the Audit Team for audit verification and validation;

observations.recommendation: c) Require the Accounting and the GSO maintain the prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile their records to prove that the assigned values to the assets per the agency’s books are reliable and accurate and to afford fair presentation of the related PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemen ted. The Accounting Office and the GSO have started maintaining the new ledger cards and recording new purchases.
observations.reason: Started recording purchases in the ledger cards; records of some assets procured long time ago are a problem while establishing the cost of PPE will require the help of competent appraisers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the pertinent governing rules and regulations provided in the Municipal Tax Code to enforce collections particularly from delinquent tenants of market stalls and business taxpayers; and shall specifically instruct the Municipal Treasurer to undertake the following measures: a) Impose penalty and apply the civil remedies provided in the pertinent Section of the Municipal Tax Ordinances.
observations.managementAction: Updating Tax Ordinances. Still unable to implement. Examination of books of delinquent business taxpayers was not conducted.
observations.reason: Still unable to enforce the collections; examination of books of delinquent business taxpayers not yet done.

observations.recommendation: b) Conduct examination of the books of accounts especially on delinquent business tax payers to ascertain, assess and collect the true correct amount of tax due from the taxpayer concerned.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemen ted. The Municipal Treasurer started enforcing the tax ordinance and some penalties applied.
observations.reason: Still in process of enforcing measures and updating records.

observations.recommendation: c) Always consider the civil remedies and penalties provided in the Municipal Tax Code to delinquent tenants of market stalls and business taxpayers; conduct examination of the books of accounts of the delinquent taxpayers to ascertain, assess, and collect the true and correct amount of tax due.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemen ted. Some actions taken but comprehensive examinations pending.
observations.reason: Process ongoing; not all delinquent accounts yet examined.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management take serious actions on strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 to prevent the related transactions from being suspended in audit and shall specifically undertake the following: a) Instruct the designated GSO and designated BAC Chairman and the BAC Secretariat to always submit perfected contracts together with the required supporting documents within the prescribed period of five (5) days from their execution/issuance.
observations.managementAction: Designated GSO and BAC Secretariat informed; some submissions made.
observations.reason: The submitted POs were not supported with the necessary supporting documents. Some POs and contracts still not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: b) Instruct the Municipal GSO to always submit copies of POs and its supporting documents within five (5) days from date of perfection.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemen ted. The GSO instructed but compliance not consistent.
observations.reason: Some POs were submitted late; also POs lacked supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of the revised IRR of R.A. 9184 particularly in resorting to alternative mode of procurement and shall undertake the following measures: a) In resorting to Shopping as the alternative mode of procurement always consider the threshold of P50,000.00 prescribed in Annex H of the IRR; b) Procurements thru alternative mode must be duly approved by Head of Procuring Entity; c) Always designate procurement support and obtain PS Certificate of non-availability of stocks to prevent disallowance; d) For purchases above threshold, conduct Competitive Bidding; e) Strict adherence in posting in PhilGEPS; f) Ensure disbursement vouchers supported with PhilGEPS posting evidence.
observations.managementAction: BAC attended RA 9184 seminar; some procedures applied; GSO and BAC Secretariat informed.
observations.reason: Not all procurements followed shopping thresholds; not all PhilGEPS postings attached to DVs; some procurement lacked PS certificates.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere on the required documentation for the payment of allowances and incentives and shall specifically undertake the following: a) Payment of RATA must be supported with DTRs; b) Payment of Hazard Pay for public health workers must be supported with DTRs and certification from Secretary of Health; c) Payment of subsistence and laundry allowances must be supported with DTRs and travel documents; d) Stop grant of additional incentive to BNS who is also a Barangay Kagawad to avoid double payment.
observations.managementAction: Implemented. DTRs were attached to claims; Municipal Accountant instructed to ensure supporting documents are complete.
observations.reason: Only some controls implemented; full compliance ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the following: 1. Require concerned official/employee in-charged in the preparation of payrolls of Job Order workers to cause the immediate compliance of the deficiencies noted in the payment of wages per attached audit working paper; 2. Require the Municipal Accountant not to process payrolls for payment of wages of Job Order workers if the required documentation is not complete and valid per COA Circular 2012-001 such as: approved Daily Time Records, verified and accepted accomplishment report, copy of Job Order Contract, locator slips.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemen ted. The concerned officials were instructed to prepare payrolls and the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness before processing.
observations.reason: Some payrolls processed without complete supporting documents; ongoing corrective actions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the immediate refund by the concerned Trainor of the respective trainings already conducted to support their claim for honoraria with Daily Time Record (DTR) duly signed by the Officer-in-Charge and duly inspected Accomplishment Report with pictures taken during the conduct of training and; b) Require the participants of the respective trainings conducted to support their claim with Certificate of Attendance duly signed by the proponents of the projects and copy of the Competency Assessment issued by the concerned agency.
observations.managementAction: Implemented. Trainors instructed to provide supporting documents; participants to submit certificates.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents complied; others pending submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the immediate refund by the concerned Sanggunian Bayan Members of the respective amount claimed to the alleged attendance to the above-mentioned PCL National Congress and meeting.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemen ted. Only two SB Members refunded their claimed amounts.
observations.reason: Only two councilors refunded; others yet to refund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the pertinent laws, rules and regulations governing Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) and undertake the following: a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and concerned office to regularly submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in Annex B of COA Circular 2012-002 to the COA Audit Team of the LGU; b) Annually prepare the LDRRMFIP which shall present the 30% QRF and 70% preparedness and mitigation fund and include under a separate caption the list of projects charged to unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; c) Stop sending Job Order personnel to attend seminars and trainings charged against the LDRRMF or to any fund of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Not implemen ted. Concerned office was required to comply; LDRRMFIP and reports to be prepared; reiterated in audit observation no. 6 of current report.
observations.reason: Reports and LDRRMFIP submission still pending; job orders attendance issue reiterated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the following: 1. Cause the immediate creation/establishment of the GAD Focal Point System; 2. Prepare annual GAD Plan and Budget and have it reviewed and duly approved by the DILG Regional Office and incorporate in the Annual Budget with equivalent cost of 5% of its total amount; 3. Prepare and submit to the Audit Team GAD accomplishment report based on the reviewed and duly approved GAD Plan and Budget to ensure implementation of GAD PPAs which are relevant to the identified gender issues.
observations.managementAction: Implemented. GAD Focal Point System creation/establishment prepared with guidance of DILG; GAD Plan prepared and submitted for review; accomplishment report for CY 2016 complied.
observations.reason: Preparation and submission done; review and approval by DILG pending confirmation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere with the required payment process and stop the practice of reimbursement and strict adherence to procurement law, and to pay directly by checks to suppliers except for small value items payable from petty cash.
observations.managementAction: Implemented. Management stopped reimbursement practice except for small value items; enjoined to promote transparency.
observations.reason: As much as possible management to stop the practice; some reimbursements still being regularized.

observations.recommendation: a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to be serious in complying the audit recommendations and submission of accounts within the prescribed period of ten (10) days after the end of each month; b) Submit other needed reports required by the audit team as representative of the Commission on Audit, to facilitate on time audit and verification on the propriety and validity of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemen ted. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer were instructed to comply and submit accounts within prescribed periods; some submissions done.
observations.reason: Submission of reports still irregular; this has been a recurring observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to COA rules in the recording of purchased supplies and materials and equipment and to undertake the following: a) instruct the Municipal GSO and Accountant to strictly adhere to the established rules and regulations in the recording of purchased supplies and materials and equipment and maintenance of supplies ledger cards, stock cards and property cards; b) Instruct the Municipal GSO to prepare Purchase Request for purchases of supplies and materials based on approved APP; c) Instruct the Municipal GSO and Accountant to have all issuances of supplies and materials covered with Requisition Issue Slips (RIS) to support the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI).
observations.managementAction: Partially implemen ted. Accounting Office and GSO in process of putting in place the inventory records; GSO instructed to prepare PR and RIS.
observations.reason: Records being set up; some prior purchases lacked documentation; reconciliation ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 77-61 in controlling the use of government vehicles, to establish transparency, validity and propriety of the expenditures, and to specifically instruct the concerned officers and employees to comply with the deficiencies noted in the transactions to avoid being suspended and/or disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: On-going verification of the old documents; management instructed drivers to accomplish trip tickets and submit monthly reports.
observations.reason: Verification and enforcement ongoing; some historical non-compliances remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to R.A. 9003 and develop and implement the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan that shall ensure the long-term management of solid waste, integrate various solid waste management plans of the barangays and undertake the following: activate the SWM Board, submit and implement MSWMP, adopt measures to promote solid waste management, monitor implementation and adopt revenue-generating measures to promote viability of SWM Plan.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemen ted. LGU formulated a solid waste management plan but failed to implement segregation of waste; 10-year plan prepared and endorsed to NSWMC; procurement for equipment prioritized in subsequent budgets.
observations.reason: Non-implementation due to delayed approval of CY2016 budget and lack of equipment; activities still pending implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for management to exhaust all remedies to determine the validity of certain payable accounts. Those payable accounts without supporting documents as well as valid claimants to the unpaid DVs shall be reverted to Other Payable accounts for proper action.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed payable accounts and on-going process to determine validity.
observations.reason: Some payable accounts established void without supporting documents; ongoing verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should initiate action for collection of overdue receivables pursuant to COA Circular No. 91-368 dated December 19, 1991. We also recommend that an effective billing and collection system be adopted in accordance with existing procedures in order that past due receivables could be collected within a relatively short period of time.
observations.managementAction: Management is in the process of complying with the Audit Recommendations and verifying old documents supporting the accounts.
observations.reason: On-going process of complying with the recommendations; verification of supporting old documents still in process.
